verdicts.reason: The `_safeHex` function effectively sanitizes color inputs, preventing injection vulnerabilities in SVG fill attributes, and all other correctness concerns, including chart data parsing, edge cases, and previous fixes, are adequately addressed.

The _safeHex function effectively sanitizes user-controlled theme color inputs, preventing SVG attribute injection. All other chart data (labels, values) are properly escaped or validated, and the feature adheres to established architectural constraints regarding innerHTML usage.
The chart feature is well-designed with clear syntax, comprehensive error handling, and good theme integration, making it intuitive for first-time users.
Chart support is a fundamental and frequently needed feature for any presentation tool, significantly enhancing its utility.
verdicts.evidence: Most presentation tools (PowerPoint, Google Slides, Keynote) include built-in chart functionality. This feature directly addresses a core user need for data visualization in presentations, making Markdown Deck a more complete and useful tool.

verdicts.reason: The inline SVG charts with CSV-like input, theme-aware colors, and robust PPTX export as images provide a unique, integrated, and 'oh, nice' experience for quick data visualization within markdown presentations, differentiating it from generic chart tools.
verdicts.evidence: Chart.js, Google Charts (as external libraries/services); Marp, reveal.js (as presentation tools lacking this specific inline, zero-dep, theme-aware chart integration)

verdicts.reason: No documented lessons or architectural constraints were violated. The deliverable adheres to established patterns and includes fixes for prior escalations.
